title: Glasses-free 3D display with ultrawide viewing range using deep learning
methods: physically accurate binocular geometric modelling (6D pose matrices)
ocular geometric encoding via reverse perspective transformation
structured loss optimization (L1 intensity + mutual-exclusion + low-frequency regularization)
Malus's law multilayer LCD phase modulation
frustum-based calibration via reversible light-path principle
least-squares regression for camera-to-light-field projection matrix
core_conclusion: EyeReal overcomes the fundamental space-bandwidth product trade-off in 3D display optics by dynamically concentrating limited optical information around the viewer's eyes in real time using a deep-learning model with physically accurate binocular geometric encoding. The result is a desktop-scale, full-parallax, glasses-free 3D display with seamless viewing beyond 100 degrees at over 50 Hz and 1920x1080 resolution on consumer LCD hardware — more than 1000 times larger than prior holographic displays with comparable angular range, while eliminating IPD mismatch and vergence-accommodation conflict.
why_it_matters: Breaks the static SBP constraint via proactive dynamic allocation rather than optical engineering; achieves real-time full-HD performance on consumer hardware; delivers all three parallax types (stereo, motion, focal) simultaneously at desktop scale; eliminates the two primary XR discomfort sources (VAC and IPD mismatch); fully open codebase and dataset released.
why_nature: Reframes a hard physical optics limit (Lagrange invariant / SBP) as a computational optimization problem — a cross-disciplinary conceptual shift; prototype-validated at full scale with photographic evidence; broad societal impact across displays, VR/AR, education, and industrial design; open and reproducible.
added_value_to_my_research: Differentiable geometric pipeline template -> jointly optimize spatial weighting functions and prediction model parameters in GeoAI (analogous to jointly optimizing LCD layer distances and FCN weights)

Physics-based structured loss design (intensity + mutual-exclusion + low-frequency regularization) -> template for spatiotemporal kernel density estimation with mass-conservation and autocorrelation soft constraints
Ocular geometric encoding (pose-conditioned view-agnostic representation) -> viewshed/line-of-sight encoding for facility-siting and visual-accessibility extensions of 2SFCA
Dynamic bandwidth concentration principle -> spatially adaptive resolution for remote-sensing data fusion guided by model uncertainty or task relevance
Resistive-memory NeRF inference efficiency -> hardware co-design direction for scaling agent-based Monte Carlo / SIR spatial simulations

related.title: Differentiable visual computing for inverse problems and machine learning
related.relation: provides differentiable rendering framework applicable to end-to-end optimization of EyeReal's physical parameters and network jointly

related.title: Photorealistic 3D Holographic Display with Natural Defocus Effect
related.relation: wave-optics defocus engine that could replace EyeReal's phase-stack focal model for physically accurate depth-of-field

related.title: Efficient and accurate neural-field reconstruction using resistive memory
related.relation: resistive-memory NeRF as efficient scene representation for multi-user EyeReal extension

related.title: Scalable optical learning operator
related.relation: scalable optical compute operator related to EyeReal's light-field processing and a template for pose-conditioned geometric encoding

related.title: Optical generative models
related.relation: generative optical computation paradigm linking EyeReal's dynamic SBP principle to broader bandwidth-constrained inference

related.title: Reusability report: Unpaired deep-learning approaches for holographic image reconstruction
related.relation: unpaired deep learning for holographic reconstruction — methodologically related to EyeReal's unsupervised phase decomposition

figures.caption: Schematic comparison of holographic, view-segmented, view-dense, and EyeReal displays under limited SBP — illustrates how EyeReal dynamically concentrates optical information around the binocular region

EyeReal system architecture: ocular modelling setup (a), LCD-stack prototype (b), Malus's law phase modulation schematic (c), binocular correspondence reconstruction (d), and the FCN decomposition network with skip connections (e)

Full-parallax autostereoscopic demonstrations: synthetic cityscape (a), real-world shoe-rack scene (b), omnidirectional motion parallax on a hot-dog object (c), focal stack of orchids (d), and physical prototype photographs of a red car showing stereo and focal parallax (e,f)

Multi-level performance benchmarking: local spatial comparison vs. view-segmented automultiscopy (a); generalization to unseen scenes and head poses with 6,000 samples (b); global spatial comparison vs. IVD/NVD baselines (c,d); runtime comparison showing 1-2 orders of magnitude speedup (e); focal discrimination curves (f); PSNR heatmap across ~600,000 randomly sampled viewpoints (g)
